jokes.text: Ew. What are you all doing here?
jokes.setup: Family gathering with excited faces to give David a surprise
jokes.punchline: David's immediate disgust at seeing his family together
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - David's automatic revulsion at family attention is both economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Judging from the looks on your faces, I don't want it.
jokes.setup: Family says they have a surprise
jokes.punchline: David pre-emptively rejects the surprise based on their expressions
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing David's pessimism and ability to read social cues for self-preservation

jokes.text: Why would Patrick do that? / That was my first thought.
jokes.setup: David receives what appears to be a celebration gift
jokes.punchline: Both David and his mother immediately question Patrick's judgment
jokes.explanation: Nice mother-son synchronization showing shared skepticism about romantic gestures

jokes.text: Four whole months, David. / How 'bout that?
jokes.setup: Parents celebrating what they think is a relationship milestone
jokes.punchline: The patronizing tone reveals how low their expectations are for David
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - parents being proud of David's four-month relationship reveals their low expectations

jokes.text: I had a... very intimate connection with Tony, for several years. / She was your pen pal, David. / She was in a penitentiary, dear.
jokes.setup: David claims a longer relationship to defend himself
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was correspondence with a female prisoner
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke - each revelation makes David's 'relationship' more pathetic while staying true to character

jokes.text: Okay, first of all, no one's sharing the cookie.
jokes.setup: Large celebration cookie that could feed 10 people
jokes.punchline: David's immediate possessiveness over food
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - David's food hoarding instincts override all social norms

jokes.text: which is why I would rather not subject him to eating charred meat with this group of carnies.
jokes.setup: David explaining why Patrick shouldn't come to barbecue
jokes.punchline: Refers to his own family as 'carnies'
jokes.explanation: Excellent insult that's both specific and character-appropriate - David's snobbery expressed through carnival metaphor

jokes.text: Carnies are people too.
jokes.setup: David calls family 'carnies'
jokes.punchline: Johnny's earnest defense of carnival workers instead of his family
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction - Johnny misses that he should be offended by the family insult

jokes.text: Perhaps it's this nay-saying reticence that caused your past relationships to... Fall apart. / ... oxidize. / Oxidize.
jokes.setup: Moira about to say relationships 'fall apart'
jokes.punchline: Johnny suggests 'oxidize' as a fancier word, Moira accepts it
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Johnny trying to match Moira's vocabulary level with an absurd word choice

jokes.text: Oh yeah, it's soft.
jokes.setup: David walking away from family argument
jokes.punchline: David commenting on cookie texture while ignoring family drama
jokes.explanation: Nice character beat showing David's priorities - food over family drama

jokes.text: First of all, a cookie is always a big deal, especially when that cookie just alerted my entire family to the fact that this is officially the longest relationship I've ever had.
jokes.setup: Patrick dismissing the cookie as no big deal
jokes.punchline: David's intense cookie philosophy and relationship anxiety
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's food obsession combined with relationship anxiety

jokes.text: If we throw a... if we throw a renaissance fair every month, I just feel like we might be tempting fate.
jokes.setup: David explaining why monthly celebrations are too much
jokes.punchline: Comparing anniversary celebration to a renaissance fair
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor that's both specific to David's dramatic worldview and genuinely funny

jokes.text: The last time I heard that, I was dating a birthday clown who painted my face in the night, and was literally never seen from again.
jokes.setup: Patrick telling David to trust people
jokes.punchline: David's bizarre and specific bad experience with trust
jokes.explanation: Exceptional joke - perfectly absurd backstory that feels completely plausible for David's character while being genuinely surprising

jokes.text: Okay, first of all, let it be known that supporting Julia Stiles is never going overboard.
jokes.setup: Patrick suggesting Julia Stiles marathon might be too much
jokes.punchline: David's passionate defense of Julia Stiles
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's unexpected pop culture loyalties and dramatic declarations

jokes.text: He was never found. / Probably dead.
jokes.setup: Patrick joking about the birthday clown story
jokes.punchline: David's matter-of-fact assumption the clown died
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the absurdity while staying true to David's dramatic pessimism

jokes.text: So David's at work and you had nobody else to talk to? / What? / That's...
jokes.setup: Alexis asking Stevie for girl talk advice
jokes.punchline: Stevie pointing out she's clearly last choice, Alexis realizing how it sounds
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction showing Stevie's directness cutting through Alexis's social blindness

jokes.text: It's a tactic, Stevie. You send someone a random text message to get their attention, and then the next thing you know, you're talking again.
jokes.setup: Explaining whether Ted's text was accidental
jokes.punchline: Alexis revealing sophisticated manipulation tactics as common knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's expertise in romantic manipulation

jokes.text: This is why I choose not to be in a relationship.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining complex dating manipulation tactics
jokes.punchline: Stevie's dry conclusion about avoiding relationships entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie reaction - deadpan conclusion that dating is too complicated to bother with

jokes.text: I used to text Zac Efron just like a question mark whenever I wanted a booty call. Poor thing would be like buzzing my apartment before I even pressed send.
jokes.setup: Discussing text manipulation tactics
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually claiming she had Zac Efron on call for sex
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis humble-brag that's both absurd and delivered with perfect casual confidence

jokes.text: You're like super smart, and pretty in like a... breezy, non-threatening sort of way.
jokes.setup: Alexis complimenting Rachel
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that reveals Alexis's competitive nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - thinks she's being nice while being unconsciously condescending

jokes.text: No, I'm sorry, I just had an image of you cooking.
jokes.setup: Johnny talking about his grilling skills
jokes.punchline: Moira can't even imagine Johnny cooking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the family dynamics and Johnny's domestic incompetence

jokes.text: Do you know that I've been given three different aprons because of my barbecue skills? Let's see, I've got License to Grill, I've got Working Grill, oh and I have Grills Just Wanna Have Fun.
jokes.setup: Roland bragging about his grilling expertise
jokes.punchline: Three terrible pun aprons as evidence of skill
jokes.explanation: Typical Roland character comedy - dad joke puns delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: I've got the perfect apron for ya, Poor Little Rich Grill. / I'll get it made up.
jokes.setup: Johnny expressing doubt about the grill
jokes.punchline: Roland's custom pun apron for Johnny's situation
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that's specifically tailored to Johnny's rich-guy-struggling situation

jokes.text: When you said that you were coming back, you just meant that you weren't coming back, and that I would have to spend the afternoon searching for you on the train tracks, and among various unmarked vans.
jokes.setup: David confronting Patrick about being late
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic assumption about searching in increasingly sinister places
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing David's tendency toward dark dramatic scenarios

jokes.text: No, but there was a moment when I thought about doing it.
jokes.setup: Patrick asking if David actually searched for him
jokes.punchline: David admitting he only considered the dramatic rescue
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - dramatic in imagination but practical in reality

jokes.text: I must've been... Dracula, or a spin instructor.
jokes.setup: David wondering what he did in past life to deserve bad relationships
jokes.punchline: Equating vampire with fitness instructor as equally evil
jokes.explanation: Exceptional joke - perfectly captures David's worldview where fitness culture is genuinely evil, delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: I must've been... Dracula, or a spin instructor.
jokes.setup: David wondering what he did in past life to deserve bad relationships
jokes.punchline: Equating vampire with fitness instructor as ultimate evil
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unexpected comparison that perfectly captures David's worldview and fitness phobia

jokes.text: Well, hopefully not all that I am, I mean that would be...
jokes.setup: Moira saying Patrick sees David for all that he is
jokes.punchline: David's horror at the thought of being fully known
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's self-awareness and fear of intimacy

jokes.text: Well, hopefully not all that I am, I mean that would be...
jokes.setup: Moira says Patrick sees David for all that he is
jokes.punchline: David's horrified reaction to being fully known
jokes.explanation: Relatable relationship anxiety about being truly known

jokes.text: No, I draw the line at sing-along.
jokes.setup: Learning Patrick is bringing guitar for potential sing-along
jokes.punchline: David's firm boundary on acceptable barbecue activities
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - David willing to compromise on many things but not group singing

jokes.text: I'm also glad I did. It's good to see you. It's also good to see you.
jokes.setup: Alexis and Ted's awkward reunion
jokes.punchline: Repetitive pleasantries revealing their discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good awkward beat showing their stilted interaction

jokes.text: Hello dummy, why do you think I'm here?
jokes.setup: Ted asking why Alexis came instead of texting
jokes.punchline: Alexis admitting she came because of the 'accidental' text tactic
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis being unusually direct about her manipulation tactics

jokes.text: Where are we, Barcelona?
jokes.setup: Johnny wanting to eat at 8pm
jokes.punchline: Roland's confusion about late European dining customs
jokes.explanation: Decent culture clash joke showing Roland's limited worldview

jokes.text: Cut to, I've ruined my sliders! / Cut to, how does this thing work?
jokes.setup: Johnny left alone with the grill
jokes.punchline: Johnny using film terminology while panicking about cooking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's tendency to narrate his life like a movie

jokes.text: Okay, just so you know, that's not really a compliment, but thank you.
jokes.setup: Moira saying David accomplished something all by himself
jokes.punchline: David recognizing the backhanded nature of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment showing David understanding his family's low expectations

jokes.text: I assumed that, based on your face, and... body.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she's usually the one being chased
jokes.punchline: Alexis's matter-of-fact assessment based purely on looks
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis moment showing her casual objectification disguised as compliment

jokes.text: No, John, no, we're not doing Pat.
jokes.setup: Patrick and Johnny bonding, Johnny calling him Pat
jokes.punchline: David firmly rejecting the nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character control moment - David managing his relationship boundaries

jokes.text: Okay, please never say titillating when referring to my relationship. / Oh, can I though? / No.
jokes.setup: Moira using the word 'titillating' about David's relationship
jokes.punchline: David's horror and Moira's genuine interest in using the word
jokes.explanation: Good family cringe moment - Moira's vocabulary choices horrifying David

jokes.text: Uh, I'm sorry you have a fiancée?
jokes.setup: Learning Patrick was engaged to Rachel
jokes.punchline: David's stunned, delayed reaction phrased as a question
jokes.explanation: Perfect shocked reaction - the delayed processing and uncertain phrasing sells the devastation

jokes.text: I glanced down at my plate for two seconds.
jokes.setup: Dramatic revelation happening during dinner
jokes.punchline: David's bewilderment at how quickly things went wrong
jokes.explanation: Good understatement showing David's disbelief at the timing of the revelation

jokes.text: That is quite possibly one of the most beautiful things I've ever heard anyone say. Um... outside of the 'Downton Christmas Special'.
jokes.setup: Patrick's heartfelt romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: David comparing it to Downton Abbey as higher praise
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - David's inability to express emotion without pop culture references, even in intimate moments

jokes.text: More than one, um, and some potato salad, and I think there were some other sides on the table, but I couldn't see, so maybe just a smattering of everything.
jokes.setup: Patrick offering to get David food after emotional conversation
jokes.punchline: David's detailed food requests as way to avoid emotional processing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David using food focus to cope with emotional overwhelm

jokes.text: Well, I don't know, he had 6 sliders. I'm assuming he'll be falling asleep soon.
jokes.setup: Question about when David might be ready to talk
jokes.punchline: Alexis's practical assessment based on his eating
jokes.explanation: Good sibling observation about David's emotional eating patterns

jokes.text: but at the same time, he's been watching that show for 3 hours and my phone's in there.
jokes.setup: David upset and watching TV in his room
jokes.punchline: Alexis's concern about her phone access outweighing David's emotional state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis moment - genuine concern mixed with self-centered priorities
